category_title: Students with untenable mainstream histories exhibiting workplace-related non-compliance like lateness
minimal_context: The student is participating in an extended committee role-play to prepare for a meeting organized with the college principal about a fundraising event.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for the committee role-play. Please get ready for the meeting organized with the college principal regarding our fundraising event.

STUDENT: I don't want to be here. This curriculum is irrelevant.

TUTOR: Please try to focus on the task.

STUDENT: It's too hard. I'm only here because school is a shelter from unemployment anyway.

TUTOR: We need to get through this work. Please take your seat for the meeting.

STUDENT: (Raises decibels) I'm bringing someone else to the meeting with me! I'm not doing this alone!

options.A: Incorporate selected poems aimed at promoting reflection and the reinforcement of mindfulness concepts during the committee role-play.
options.B: Provide the student with the opportunity to complete all of their committee role-play preparation on a computer or laptop.
options.C: Make use of weaker gravity moves appealing to professional standards, such as reminding the student of workplace expectations regarding meeting attendance.
options.D: Allow the student complete freedom and autonomy in how they choose to approach the tasks for the committee role-play.
